title: Search underway for driver who led cops on a chase through Everett, Malden, Stoneham
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Driver with multiple arrest warrants led police on chase through Everett, Malden, and Stoneham, striking a State Police cruiser.
The suspect, wanted in a drug investigation, abandoned the vehicle in Stoneham and escaped on foot with a passenger.
Authorities continue to search for the driver, urging the public to report any information related to the incident.

title: Ex-Patriots HC Jerod Mayo has a new job away from football
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jerod Mayo joins Fifth Down Capital as managing director, marking his return to the business sector.
Mayo, former Patriots head coach, was fired after a 4-13 season and two six-game losing streaks.
Fifth Down Capital, based in Needham, invests in technology, healthcare, and consumer/retail sectors.

title: Mass. man reports beating Mount Everest speed climbing record
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Tyler Andrews of Concord set a new speed record for climbing Mount Everest with supplemental oxygen, summiting in 9 hours, 55 minutes.
Andrews's climb broke a 23-year-old record, with his entire round-trip lasting 16 hours, 32 minutes, pending verification by Nepal officials.
Previously holding over 100 records, Andrews has attempted Everest's speed record seven times, dedicating this climb to late friend Greg Krupa.

title: You may complain about the T, but a new ranking says it’s one of the best in the U.S.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston public transit ranked third best in U.S. by Travel + Leisure, highlighting MBTA's extensive options.
The MBTA praised for bus network reaching train-inaccessible neighborhoods and Commuter Rail connecting to wider destinations.
Despite high ranking, the MBTA faces criticism for delays and disruptions during infrastructure improvements.

title: Wu gets rid of green infrastructure office, lays off director
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston Mayor Michelle Wu is eliminating the standalone Office of Green Infrastructure, incorporating it into the Streets Cabinet.
Green Infrastructure Director Max Rome has been laid off as part of the fiscal year 2027 budget changes.
Wu proposes using $70 million in reserves to address budget deficits, with plans to integrate green infrastructure citywide.

title: Boston reports uptick in mpox cases
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston Public Health Commission reports an increase in mpox cases and urges high-risk groups to get vaccinated.
Vaccinations will be offered during Boston's Pride flag-raising event at City Hall on June 1, from 1 to 4 p.m.
The mpox vaccine is 80% effective; additional clinics will be held citywide, with information on the BPHC website.

title: Citgo sign goes dark — temporarily
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: The iconic Citgo sign in Kenmore Square is being moved 120 feet east and elevated 30 feet.
The repositioning is part of the redevelopment of 660 Beacon Street and will be completed by December 2026.
The relocation involves removing the sign's letters, logos, and superstructure, followed by roof and electrical work.

title: Canton police respond to 2 ‘significant incidents’ at controversial therapy center
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Canton police responded to two incidents at the Judge Rotenberg Educational Center, a facility known for using electric shock therapy.
A May 22 incident involved a resident fleeing, prompting a search involving drones and K-9 units, halting MBTA rail traffic.
On May 27, a resident armed with glass injured a police officer, leading to two hospitalizations and potential criminal charges.

title: Here’s what Boston meteorologists are saying about ‘unsettled weather’ into next week
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Unsettled weather kicks off with several showers and strong gusty winds predicted for Friday night into Saturday.
Sunday will see brief dry periods before showers return, with a weather alert for an unusually chilly day.
Higher terrains in northern Worcester County in southwest NH may experience some wet snowflakes mixed with rain.

title: Former home-care aide charged in multimillion-dollar jewelry heist from JP home
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Cory Kisakye, 27, a former home-care aide, is charged with unarmed burglary and larceny from a Jamaica Plain home.
The burglary involved a multimillion-dollar jewelry heist, with items stolen valued from $1,000 to $850,000.
Kisakye was tracked via fingerprint and GPS data and later sold a stolen item for $11,000 in Miami.
